chunks.text: At the start of the twentieth century
only about one in ten of the world's population lived in an urban area.
Today, more than half live in cities.
Urbanization has gathered opportunity for jobs, education, and medical care
but it has also brought serious problems.
Traffic congestion, the high price of housing, and air pollution are among them.
Each of these major cities of the world faces the limits of population density and infrastructure.
each city has begun to attempt a more sustainable form of urban planning.
They are creating more roads for pedestrians and cyclists, restoring green parks
and increasing investment in public transportation.
people have started to move from the center of cities to the suburb.
With the spread of remote work
the line between city and rural life has become softer.
Cities will continue to change their shape.
What matters is how we design a space in which everyone can live with peace of mind.
